data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Ground agent: the keeper of the project's human-readable mind. You own three things and must leave each true to the project's real state before you finish:
- the blueprints (LaTeX-subset declarations + `\uses` dependency edges),
- the roadmap (the planning index that drives scheduling) ‚Äî edit it ONLY via the `"$HORIZON_BIN" roadmap` CLI (`set`/`add`/`remove`, `comment <id> --author ground` to log a key advance; pass long prose with `--summary-file` and `--author` on add/set), NEVER by hand-editing the YAML, which corrupts it; the CLI quotes safely,
- memory: durable facts/conventions/dead ends, kept as `memory` inbox items (`horizon inbox add --kind memory --author ground`); prune stale ones with `inbox archive`.
You are not the prover. Keep this session short and operational: understand recent Horizon work, keep the workspace clean, keep the roadmap/blueprints honest, and leave orientation notes that help Horizon find the right context quickly.

# This is a RECONCILE Ground
A Horizon session just ran. Do a bounded review of its report and project diff (the `project-git` skill), then reconcile blueprints/roadmap/memory with what actually changed. Focus on correctness, consistency, and workspace hygiene; avoid long proof search or exhaustive theorem triage.

The Horizon agent owns deep formalization and strategy. You are its supervisor and janitor: check that the project is not drifting, that definitions in Lean and blueprint mean the same thing, that dependency edges are represented, that references are present and organized, and that no strategy is mathematically bogus or merely cosmetic.

How to work:
- On a reconcile round, review the Horizon agent's latest work via its report and the project diff (see the `project-git` skill ‚Äî projects have no root `.git`; the workspace keeps one out-of-tree ledger, so diff a project by pathspec / by the session's commit trailers). Check that the Lean definitions truly match the blueprint statements, that its reasoning is sound, and that it left no stray files or directories.
- Reconcile blueprints, roadmap status, and memory with what actually changed. Fix blueprint errors, retrieve and add missing references, and keep the DAG dependency-correct. Prefer narrow checks and representative builds over full-workspace audits unless there is evidence of an environment problem.
- Maintain the inbox as a small working set: archive stale or consumed items that are no longer key context, keep roughly 10 open memory items and 3-4 open info items at most, and consolidate duplicate notes before adding new persistent context.
- When you find a flaw ‚Äî a wrong proof, a Lean/blueprint mismatch, a dead end ‚Äî raise an inbox item or comment rather than silently papering over it (see the horizon-inbox skill); use `--author ground` for inbox items/comments you create. ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, roadmap summaries, and reports in Markdown ‚Äî short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. The inbox is durable coordination, not a progress log: prefer one concise issue/hint over many comments, retag or merge duplicates, and add a concise conclusion comment when an item records completed work.
- You are the inbox's keeper: it is YOUR job to keep it tidy. `archive` stale, superseded, consumed, or no-longer-relevant items so they leave the default view; use `complete` only when an item records work that was genuinely concluded and merits a conclusion comment. Conclusion comments should state the mathematical result in human-readable language (LaTeX for formulas), not just a code changelog (see the horizon-inbox skill).
- When you make a change the human should know about ‚Äî you restructured projects, took an unexpected direction, or hit something worth flagging ‚Äî tell them with an `info` inbox item (`horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground`). It is purely a notice and never affects what runs.
- You do not create tasks: tasks are the human's way to launch sessions (and are inferred on demand from a roadmap milestone via `horizon run <roadmap_id>`). To propose work, open an inbox item addressed to the human (`horizon inbox add --to human`); you may `horizon task comment` to suggest an edit, but never add/edit/remove tasks.
- Delegate to subagents for bounded review and upkeep (blueprint review, diff analysis, reference lookup), preferring a smaller/cheaper model for mechanical checks.
- Keep the roadmap as the project's mathematical status: the main theorems and infrastructure already formalized and still to build, each a milestone that guides the work ‚Äî it is a map, not a task queue. Update item statuses to match the real Lean/blueprint state, and add milestones for major results that emerge. Before closing/blocking/rejecting a roadmap item, add a concise roadmap comment explaining the evidence and checks. If you comment on a task whose status should be changed by the human, explicitly name the linked `roadmap_refs` that should move with it and the matching status/comment to apply. Marking an item active is guidance, not a command, and does not by itself launch work: a human runs a milestone with `horizon run <roadmap_id>`. If you think work should start, say so in an inbox item to the human.
- Treat the opened inbox below as input for this round only. Preserve frozen/protected state; if an edit would violate it, leave it and flag it.

Blueprint constraints (legible and pure, but mathematically complete ‚Äî see the blueprint-conventions skill):
- One declaration's worth per node: a short statement + a COMPLETE proof (not a sketch). Keep nodes small by splitting a hard step into its own `\uses`-linked lemma, never by abbreviating the proof to a hand-wave.
- Pure mathematics only: no Lean tactics, no semi-Lean pseudocode, no project history. Do not restate Lean source as prose. Add a `\uses` edge rather than re-explaining a dependency.
- The roadmap is an index, not a journal: one line of summary per item; link, don't recount.
- Memory holds only what is non-obvious and durable: conventions, invariants, dead ends.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` ‚Äî a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions ‚Äî The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style ‚Äî environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit ‚Äî Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` ‚Äî small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox ‚Äî Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox ‚Äî list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting ‚Äî How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session ‚Äî block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check ‚Äî Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors ‚Äî use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag ‚Äî Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) ‚Äî declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects ‚Äî via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch ‚Äî Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving ‚Äî the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git ‚Äî How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references ‚Äî The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml ‚Äî how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents ‚Äî How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work ‚Äî spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Run: 0010; focus: tasks=T12

Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).

# Roadmap
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) ƒåech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **ƒåech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC ‚Äî axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), ƒåech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` ‚Äî **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact ‚Äî **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman ¬ß5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley‚ÄìRosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (K√§hler, rigidity, Abel‚ÄìJacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** K√§hler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel‚ÄìJacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander‚ÄìBuchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends off a locus of codimension $\ge 2$:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
Milne 3.1 (codim-$\ge2$ indeterminacy, valuative) and the empty-locus extension theorem are proved; `Thm32RationalMapExtension` is sorry-free. The chain is now gated on the single remaining leaf **Milne 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): a pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy point of a map into a group scheme forces the difference-map contradiction.

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III ¬ß6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (√ó7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine ƒåech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free ƒåech engine - **Combinatorial / free ƒåech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented ƒåech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover ‚Äî unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the ƒåech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded Hilbert‚ÄìSerre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the œá-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [done/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable ‚Äî finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices ‚Äî `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` ‚Äî the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard).

Lean target is a ~3900-line ¬ß1 Altman‚ÄìKleiman base-change scaffold (`Basic.lean`), now pinned to mathlib v4.31.0 and building green (8560 jobs). One residual `sorry` (`hSupportBC`) plus deliberate `External` axioms remain.

- PIC.idcomp [blocked/proof/normal] (Picard-IdentityComponent) Identity-component group-scheme substrate (A.3 leg) - **Identity-component group-scheme substrate (A.3 leg).** Extraction subproject carrying the `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent`-based construction of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}$ and its abelian-variety structure:
$$e\in\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=\big(\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\big)^{\!\circ},\qquad \dim_k\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g .$$
Builds green (byte-identical to the AJC copies) as an FBC-free, isolated iteration baseline. All 9 residual `sorry` are **substrate-blocked**, not proof-search: the ¬ß1 group-scheme leaves (identity-component clopen / subgroup / base change) need Stacks **037Q/04KU** (connected $k$-scheme + $k$-rational section $\Rightarrow$ geometrically connected) and **02LB/04KS** clopen-descent, both absent from v4.31 Mathlib; the $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$-specific leaves (`Pic0Scheme`, `degree`, `isAbelianVariety`, `finrank_eq_genus`) inherit the FGA sorry-taint (`AJC.picrep`) and the tangent-space comparison (`AJC.pic0av`).

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint ‚Äî Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end ‚Äî keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug ‚Äî Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run ‚Äî missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors ‚Äî applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor ‚Äî Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier ‚Äî keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber ‚Äî Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever ‚Äî Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] ‚Äî Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task ‚Äî starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper ‚Äî then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits ‚Äî use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` ‚Äî it recompiles on the next run.

# Memory
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

Give every LinearEquiv a fully-typed `have` ‚Äî `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables.

# Orientation for the next agent (write `recommendation.md`)
Write at most 4 concise bullets. This file is not a strategy memo and not a list of commands. It should give Horizon quick access to context: relevant Lean files/declarations, blueprint nodes, reference locations, recent work, environment/build notes, and any Lean/blueprint consistency concern.
Avoid directive language like `do`, `do not`, `must`, `safe target`, or `avoid`, and avoid ranking targets by fear of difficulty. Prefer wording like `Useful context:` or `Relevant files:`. Encourage ambitious progress: Horizon may widen scope, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor strategy, or create tools when that is the best route. Human-facing notes go to an `info` inbox item; durable dead ends go to memory.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' ‚Äî that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session ‚Äî run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action ‚Äî never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

data.content:
---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** ‚Äî its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

data.content:
---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace ‚Äî root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** ‚Äî its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

2200	\end{theorem}
2201	
2202	\begin{proof}
2203	  \leanok
2204	  Choose an \(e\)-presentation chart \(V \ni x\) with presentation \(P\).  The
2205	  image of the closed immersion is the support of the ideal sheaf, which meets
2206	  \(V\) in the zero locus of \(I_e(V)\); and \(I_e(V) = I(\psi_P)\) by
2207	  \cref{thm:strata_ideal_eq_entry_ideal}.  A point of an affine open lies in
2208	  the zero locus of an ideal of sections iff its prime contains that ideal, so
2209	  \(x\) lies in the stratum iff \(I(\psi_P) \subseteq \mathfrak p_x\).  By
2210	  \cref{thm:entry_ideal_prime_iff} this holds iff the fibre rank of
2211	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\) at \(\mathfrak p_x\) --- the chart fibre rank at
2212	  \(x\), equal to the point rank by
2213	  \cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank} --- equals \(e\).
2214	\end{proof}
2215	
2216	\section{Representability of the rank strata}
2217	\label{sec:flatstrat_representability}
2218	
2219	The rank-\(e\) stratum of \cref{sec:flatstrat_rank_stratum} is a closed
2220	subscheme with the correct support.  This section establishes that it also
2221	has the correct functor of points --- Nitsure's local universal property:
2222	the pullback of \(\mathcal G\) along a morphism is locally free of rank
2223	\(e\) exactly when the pulled-back relation matrices vanish, exactly when
2224	the morphism factors through the stratum --- and assembles the strata over
2225	the chart loci into the locally closed pieces of the flattening
2226	stratification of a finitely presented module on a locally noetherian
2227	scheme.  Throughout, ``flat over \(T\)'' for an \(\OO_T\)-module means flat
2228	over \(T\) via the identity morphism of \(T\), in the affine-local sense of
2229	\cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}.
2230	
2231	\subsection{Pullback presentations and flatness of sections}
2232	
2233	\begin{lemma}[Finitely presented modules admit matrix presentations]
2234	  \label{lem:exists_matrix_presentation}
2235	  \lean{Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation}
2236	  \leanok
2237	  \uses{def:matrix_presentation}
2238	  Every finitely presented module \(M\) over a commutative ring \(R\) admits
2239	  a matrix presentation: there are natural numbers \(e, m\) and a matrix
2240	  presentation of \(M\) by \(e\) generators and \(m\) relations.
2241	\end{lemma}
2242	
2243	\begin{proof}
2244	  \leanok
2245	  By finite presentation there is a surjection \(\pi : R^{\oplus e} \to M\)
2246	  whose kernel \(K\) is finitely generated; choose generators
2247	  \(t_1, \dots, t_m\) of \(K \subseteq R^{\oplus e}\) and let \(\psi\) be
2248	  the \(e \times m\) matrix with columns \(t_j\).  The image of
2249	  \(\psi : R^{\oplus m} \to R^{\oplus e}\) is the submodule generated by
2250	  the columns, namely \(K = \ker \pi\), so
2251	  \(R^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} R^{\oplus e} \xrightarrow{\pi} M \to 0\)
2252	  is exact.
2253	\end{proof}
2254	
2255	\begin{lemma}[Pullback of a presentation to section modules]
2256	  \label{lem:presentation_pullback_sections}
2257	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections}
2258	  \leanok
2259	  \uses{def:matrix_presentation}
2260	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2261	  Let \(g : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes, \(\mathcal G\) a
2262	  quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module, \(V \subseteq X\) an affine open,
2263	  \(W \subseteq g^{-1}(V)\) an affine open, and \(P = (\psi, \pi)\) a matrix
2264	  presentation of \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\) by \(e\) generators and \(m\)
2265	  relations over \(\Gamma(X, V)\).  Then \(\Gamma(g^*\mathcal G, W)\) admits
2266	  a matrix presentation by \(e\) generators and \(m\) relations over
2267	  \(\Gamma(Y, W)\) whose relation matrix is the entrywise image
2268	  \(g^\sharp(\psi)\) of \(\psi\) under the induced ring map
2269	  \(g^\sharp : \Gamma(X, V) \to \Gamma(Y, W)\).
2270	\end{lemma}
2271	
2272	\begin{proof}
2273	  \leanok
2274	  \uses{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma, def:matrix_presentation_base_change}
2275	  For the affine pair \((V, W)\) the sections of the pullback module satisfy
2276	  the base-change formula
2277	  \(\Gamma(g^*\mathcal G, W) \cong
2278	    \Gamma(Y, W) \otimes_{\Gamma(X, V)} \Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\)
2279	  as \(\Gamma(Y, W)\)-modules (\cref{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma}).
2280	  Base change of \(P\) along \(g^\sharp\)
2281	  (\cref{def:matrix_presentation_base_change}) is a matrix presentation of
2282	  the right-hand side --- the pulled-back sequence stays exact by
2283	  right-exactness of the tensor product --- with relation matrix
2284	  \(g^\sharp(\psi)\), and transporting it along the base-change isomorphism
2285	  changes neither the generator count nor the relation matrix.
2286	\end{proof}
2287	
2288	\begin{lemma}[Flat sections from flatness over the identity]
2289	  \label{lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat}
2290	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id}
2291	  \leanok
2292	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2293	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be an \(\OO_T\)-module that is flat over \(T\).  Then
2294	  for every affine open \(W \subseteq T\) the section module
2295	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, W)\) is flat over \(\Gamma(T, W)\).
2296	\end{lemma}
2297	
2298	\begin{proof}
2299	  \leanok
2300	  Specialize the affine-local form of flatness over the identity to the pair
2301	  of affine opens \(U := W\) and \(W \subseteq \mathrm{id}^{-1}(U) = U\):
2302	  the module \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, W)\) is flat over \(\Gamma(T, W)\) acting
2303	  through the ring map on sections induced by the identity morphism, which
2304	  is the identity of \(\Gamma(T, W)\), so the action is the canonical one.
2305	\end{proof}
2306	
2307	\begin{lemma}[Flatness over the identity from an affine cover]
2308	  \label{lem:sheaf_flat_of_flat_charts}
2309	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts}
2310	  \leanok
2311	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2312	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_T\)-module and \((W_j)_j\) an
2313	  affine open cover of \(T\) such that each \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, W_j)\) is
2314	  flat over \(\Gamma(T, W_j)\).  Then \(\mathcal G\) is flat over \(T\).
2315	\end{lemma}
2316	
2317	\begin{proof}
2318	  \leanok
2319	  \uses{lem:flat_affine_local}
2320	  Apply the affine-locality of flatness over the base
2321	  (\cref{lem:flat_affine_local}) to the identity morphism of \(T\) with the
2322	  charts \(U_j := W_j\), \(W_j \subseteq \mathrm{id}^{-1}(U_j)\): flatness
2323	  of the section modules on the covering charts gives flatness of
2324	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, W)\) over \(\Gamma(T, U)\) for every pair of affine
2325	  opens \(W \subseteq U\), which is flatness over the identity.
2326	\end{proof}

2328	\begin{lemma}[Flatness over the base is stable under pullback]
2329	  \label{lem:sheaf_flat_pullback}
2330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.coherentSheafFlat_id_pullback}
2331	  \leanok
2332	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2333	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_T\)-module flat over \(T\),
2334	  and \(g : T' \to T\) a morphism of schemes.  Then \(g^*\mathcal G\) is
2335	  flat over \(T'\).
2336	\end{lemma}
2337	
2338	\begin{proof}
2339	  \leanok
2340	  \uses{lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat, def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma,
2341	    lem:sheaf_flat_of_flat_charts}
2342	  Cover \(T'\) by affine opens \(W\), each contained in the preimage of some
2343	  affine open \(V \subseteq T\).  For such a pair the section module
2344	  \(\Gamma(g^*\mathcal G, W) \cong
2345	    \Gamma(T', W) \otimes_{\Gamma(T, V)} \Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\)
2346	  (\cref{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma}) is a base change of the flat
2347	  module \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\) (\cref{lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat}),
2348	  hence flat over \(\Gamma(T', W)\).  The pullback \(g^*\mathcal G\) is
2349	  quasi-coherent, so \cref{lem:sheaf_flat_of_flat_charts} upgrades the cover
2350	  of flat section modules to flatness over \(T'\).
2351	\end{proof}

2353	\subsection{The rank dictionary}
2354	
2355	\begin{theorem}[Stalk rank of an affine section module]
2356	  \label{thm:stalk_rank_eq_point_rank}
2357	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank}
2358	  \leanok
2359	  \uses{def:point_rank}
2360	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_T\)-module and
2361	  \(W \subseteq T\) an affine open such that \(M := \Gamma(\mathcal G, W)\)
2362	  is flat and finite over \(R := \Gamma(T, W)\).  Let \(\mathfrak p\) be a
2363	  prime of \(R\) and \(x \in W\) the corresponding point under the canonical
2364	  identification of \(W\) with the prime spectrum of \(R\).  Then the
2365	  localization \(M_{\mathfrak p}\) is a free \(R_{\mathfrak p}\)-module
2366	  whose rank equals the point rank of \(\mathcal G\) at \(x\).
2367	\end{theorem}
2368	
2369	\begin{proof}
2370	  \leanok
2371	  \uses{def:fiber_rank, def:chart_fiber_rank,
2372	    lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}
2373	  \(M_{\mathfrak p}\) is finite and flat over the local ring
2374	  \(R_{\mathfrak p}\), hence free of some rank \(r\) (a finitely generated
2375	  flat module over a local ring is free).  Reducing modulo the maximal
2376	  ideal,
2377	  \(M \otimes_R \kappa(\mathfrak p)
2378	    \cong M_{\mathfrak p} \otimes_{R_{\mathfrak p}} \kappa(\mathfrak p)\)
2379	  has dimension \(r\) over \(\kappa(\mathfrak p)\), so \(r\) is the fiber
2380	  rank of \(M\) at \(\mathfrak p\) (\cref{def:fiber_rank}).  The prime of
2381	  \(R\) attached to the point \(x\) is \(\mathfrak p\) itself, so this fiber
2382	  rank is the chart fibre rank of \(\mathcal G\) at \(x\) computed on \(W\)
2383	  (\cref{def:chart_fiber_rank}), which equals the point rank by
2384	  \cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}.
2385	\end{proof}
2386	
2387	\begin{theorem}[Point rank of a pullback]
2388	  \label{thm:point_rank_pullback}
2389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pointRank_pullback}
2390	  \leanok
2391	  \uses{def:point_rank, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2392	  Let \(g : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal G\) a
2393	  quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module.  For every \(y \in Y\), the point rank of
2394	  \(g^*\mathcal G\) at \(y\) equals the point rank of \(\mathcal G\) at
2395	  \(g(y)\).  No flatness or finiteness hypotheses are needed.
2396	\end{theorem}
2397	
2398	\begin{proof}
2399	  \leanok
2400	  \uses{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma, thm:fiber_rank_base_change,
2401	    lem:fiber_rank_congr, lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}
2402	  Choose an affine open \(V \ni g(y)\) and an affine open \(W \ni y\) with
2403	  \(W \subseteq g^{-1}(V)\).  By the base-change formula for sections
2404	  (\cref{def:pullback_app_isoTensor_sigma}),
2405	  \(\Gamma(g^*\mathcal G, W) \cong
2406	    \Gamma(Y, W) \otimes_{\Gamma(X, V)} \Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\).  The prime
2407	  \(\mathfrak q \subseteq \Gamma(Y, W)\) of \(y\) contracts along the
2408	  induced ring map to the prime \(\mathfrak p \subseteq \Gamma(X, V)\) of
2409	  \(g(y)\).  By \cref{lem:fiber_rank_congr} and base-change invariance of
2410	  the fiber rank (\cref{thm:fiber_rank_base_change}), the fiber rank of
2411	  \(\Gamma(g^*\mathcal G, W)\) at \(\mathfrak q\) equals the fiber rank of
2412	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal G, V)\) at \(\mathfrak p\).  These are the chart fibre
2413	  ranks of \(g^*\mathcal G\) at \(y\) on \(W\) and of \(\mathcal G\) at
2414	  \(g(y)\) on \(V\), i.e.\ the two point ranks
2415	  (\cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}).
2416	\end{proof}

2418	\subsection{Factorization through the stratum}
2419	
2420	\begin{theorem}[The strata ideal dies under a flat constant-rank pullback]
2421	  \label{thm:strata_ideal_le_ker}
2422	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.strataData_le_ker}
2423	  \leanok
2424	  \uses{def:strata_data, def:charts_cover, def:coherent_sheaf_flat,
2425	    def:point_rank, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2426	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2427	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module admitting
2428	  \(e\)-presentation charts, and let \(q : T \to X\) be a morphism such that
2429	  \(q^*\mathcal G\) is flat over \(T\) and has point rank \(e\) at every
2430	  point of \(T\).  Then the strata ideal sheaf is contained in the kernel of
2431	  \(q\): for every affine open \(U \subseteq X\), every \(r \in I_e(U)\)
2432	  pulls back to \(0 \in \Gamma(T, q^{-1}(U))\).
2433	\end{theorem}
2434	
2435	\begin{proof}
2436	  \leanok
2437	  \uses{lem:charts_cover_finite, lem:presentation_pullback_sections,
2438	    lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat, thm:stalk_rank_eq_point_rank,
2439	    thm:entry_ideal_flat_vanish, lem:entry_ideal_vanish, def:strata_ideal,
2440	    lem:appLE_base_res_apply}
2441	  A section of a sheaf vanishes if it vanishes on a cover, so it suffices to
2442	  find, around each point \(t \in q^{-1}(U)\), an open on which
2443	  \(q^\sharp(r)\) restricts to \(0\).  Choose finitely many
2444	  \(e\)-presentation charts \(V_1, \dots, V_N \subseteq U\) covering \(U\),
2445	  with presentations \(P_i = (\psi_i, \pi_i)\)
2446	  (\cref{lem:charts_cover_finite}).  Given \(t\), pick \(i\) with
2447	  \(q(t) \in V_i\), and an affine open \(W \ni t\) with
2448	  \(W \subseteq q^{-1}(V_i)\).
2449	
2450	  By \cref{lem:presentation_pullback_sections} the module
2451	  \(\Gamma(q^*\mathcal G, W)\) has a matrix presentation by \(e\) generators
2452	  with relation matrix \(q^\sharp(\psi_i)\); in particular it is finite over
2453	  \(\Gamma(T, W)\), and it is flat by
2454	  \cref{lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat}.  At every prime of
2455	  \(\Gamma(T, W)\) its localization is free of rank equal to the point rank
2456	  of \(q^*\mathcal G\) at the corresponding point
2457	  (\cref{thm:stalk_rank_eq_point_rank}), which is \(e\) by hypothesis.  By
2458	  \cref{thm:entry_ideal_flat_vanish} the relation matrix vanishes:
2459	  \(q^\sharp(\psi_i) = 0\).  Hence the entry ideal \(I(\psi_i)\) is
2460	  contained in the kernel of
2461	  \(q^\sharp : \Gamma(X, V_i) \to \Gamma(T, W)\)
2462	  (\cref{lem:entry_ideal_vanish}).  The strata-ideal condition
2463	  (\cref{def:strata_ideal}) gives \(r|_{V_i} \in I(\psi_i)\), and the maps
2464	  induced by \(q\) on sections commute with restriction
2465	  (\cref{lem:appLE_base_res_apply}), so
2466	  \(q^\sharp(r)|_W = q^\sharp(r|_{V_i}) = 0\), as required.
2467	\end{proof}
2468	
2469	\begin{theorem}[Unique factorization through the rank-\(e\) stratum]
2470	  \label{thm:stratum_lift_unique}
2471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.existsUnique_stratumLift}
2472	  \leanok
2473	  \uses{def:stratum, def:stratum_immersion, def:charts_cover,
2474	    def:coherent_sheaf_flat, def:point_rank, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2475	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2476	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module admitting
2477	  \(e\)-presentation charts, and let \(q : T \to X\) be a morphism such that
2478	  \(q^*\mathcal G\) is flat over \(T\) and has point rank \(e\) at every
2479	  point of \(T\).  Then \(q\) factors uniquely through the closed immersion
2480	  of the rank-\(e\) stratum \(Z_e \hookrightarrow X\).
2481	\end{theorem}
2482	
2483	\begin{proof}
2484	  \leanok
2485	  \uses{thm:strata_ideal_le_ker, def:strata_data}
2486	  The stratum \(Z_e\) is the closed subscheme cut out by the strata
2487	  ideal-sheaf datum (\cref{def:strata_data}), and the kernel of its
2488	  immersion is exactly that ideal sheaf.  A morphism into \(X\) factors
2489	  through the closed subscheme cut out by a quasi-coherent ideal sheaf if
2490	  and only if that ideal sheaf is contained in the kernel of the morphism,
2491	  and then the factorization is unique because a closed immersion is a
2492	  monomorphism.  By \cref{thm:strata_ideal_le_ker} the strata ideal sheaf is
2493	  contained in the kernel of \(q\).
2494	\end{proof}
2495	
2496	\begin{theorem}[The rank-\(e\) stratum flattens \(\mathcal G\)]
2497	  \label{thm:stratum_flattens}
2498	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.coherentSheafFlat_stratum}
2499	  \leanok
2500	  \uses{def:stratum, def:stratum_immersion, def:charts_cover,
2501	    def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2502	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2503	  Let \(\mathcal G\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module admitting
2504	  \(e\)-presentation charts, and \(\iota : Z_e \to X\) the closed immersion
2505	  of the rank-\(e\) stratum.  Then \(\iota^*\mathcal G\) is flat over
2506	  \(Z_e\).
2507	\end{theorem}
2508	
2509	\begin{proof}
2510	  \leanok
2511	  \uses{lem:presentation_pullback_sections, thm:strata_ideal_eq_entry_ideal,
2512	    def:entry_ideal, lem:presentation_free_of_vanish,
2513	    lem:sheaf_flat_of_flat_charts, def:strata_data}
2514	  The preimages \(\iota^{-1}(V)\) of the \(e\)-presentation charts \(V\)
2515	  form an affine open cover of \(Z_e\) (a closed immersion is an affine
2516	  morphism, and the charts cover \(X\)).  Fix a chart \(V\) with
2517	  presentation \(P = (\psi, \pi)\).  By
2518	  \cref{lem:presentation_pullback_sections} the section module
2519	  \(\Gamma(\iota^*\mathcal G, \iota^{-1}(V))\) has a matrix presentation
2520	  with relation matrix \(\iota^\sharp(\psi)\).  Every entry of \(\psi\) lies
2521	  in the entry ideal \(I(\psi)\) (\cref{def:entry_ideal}), which equals the
2522	  strata ideal \(I_e(V)\) (\cref{thm:strata_ideal_eq_entry_ideal}); and the
2523	  kernel of \(\iota^\sharp : \Gamma(X, V) \to \Gamma(Z_e, \iota^{-1}(V))\)
2524	  is exactly \(I_e(V)\), the ideal cutting out the subscheme.  Hence
2525	  \(\iota^\sharp(\psi) = 0\), so the section module is free
2526	  (\cref{lem:presentation_free_of_vanish}), in particular flat, over
2527	  \(\Gamma(Z_e, \iota^{-1}(V))\).  Conclude by
2528	  \cref{lem:sheaf_flat_of_flat_charts}.
2529	\end{proof}

2531	\subsection{The chart locus}
2532	
2533	\begin{theorem}[Chart supply on a locally noetherian scheme]
2534	  \label{thm:presentation_chart_exists}
2535	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_presentationChart_mem}
2536	  \leanok
2537	  \uses{def:presentation_chart, def:point_rank}
2538	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2539	  Let \(S\) be a locally noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a finitely presented
2540	  \(\OO_S\)-module.  Every point \(s \in S\) lies in an \(e\)-presentation
2541	  chart for \(\F\) with \(e\) the point rank of \(\F\) at \(s\).
2542	\end{theorem}
2543	
2544	\begin{proof}
2545	  \leanok
2546	  \uses{lem:affine_finite_sections_nhds, thm:nakayama_prolongation,
2547	    lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}
2548	  Choose an affine open \(V \ni s\) on which \(M := \Gamma(\F, V)\) is a
2549	  finite module over \(R := \Gamma(S, V)\)
2550	  (\cref{lem:affine_finite_sections_nhds}).  Since \(S\) is locally
2551	  noetherian, \(R\) is a noetherian ring, so the finite module \(M\) is
2552	  finitely presented.  Let \(\mathfrak p\) be the prime of \(s\) in \(R\)
2553	  and \(e\) the fiber rank of \(M\) at \(\mathfrak p\); by
2554	  \cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}, \(e\) is the point rank of
2555	  \(\F\) at \(s\).  The Nakayama prolongation
2556	  (\cref{thm:nakayama_prolongation}) supplies \(g \notin \mathfrak p\) such
2557	  that every localization of \(M\) away from \(g\) admits a matrix
2558	  presentation by \(e\) generators; by quasi-coherence
2559	  \(\Gamma(\F, D(g))\) is such a localization, so the basic open
2560	  \(D(g) \subseteq V\) is an \(e\)-presentation chart, and it contains
2561	  \(s\) because \(g \notin \mathfrak p\).
2562	\end{proof}

2564	\begin{definition}[Chart locus]
2565	  \label{def:chart_locus}
2566	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.chartLocus}
2567	  \leanok
2568	  \uses{def:presentation_chart}
2569	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2570	  The \emph{\(e\)-chart locus} \(W_e \subseteq S\) of an \(\OO_S\)-module
2571	  \(\F\) is the union of all \(e\)-presentation charts for \(\F\); it is an
2572	  open subset of \(S\).
2573	\end{definition}
2574	
2575	\begin{lemma}[The chart locus contains the rank-\(e\) locus]
2576	  \label{lem:chart_locus_contains_rank_locus}
2577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.mem_chartLocus_of_pointRank_eq}
2578	  \leanok
2579	  \uses{def:chart_locus, def:point_rank}
2580	  Let \(S\) be locally noetherian and \(\F\) finitely presented.  Every
2581	  point of point rank \(e\) lies in \(W_e\).
2582	\end{lemma}
2583	
2584	\begin{proof}
2585	  \leanok
2586	  \uses{thm:presentation_chart_exists}
2587	  \cref{thm:presentation_chart_exists} puts such a point inside an
2588	  \(e\)-presentation chart.
2589	\end{proof}
2590	
2591	\begin{lemma}[Rank bound on the chart locus]
2592	  \label{lem:chart_locus_rank_bound}
2593	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pointRank_le_of_mem_chartLocus}
2594	  \leanok
2595	  \uses{def:chart_locus, def:point_rank}
2596	  Let \(\F\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.  Every point of \(W_e\)
2597	  has point rank at most \(e\).
2598	\end{lemma}
2599	
2600	\begin{proof}
2601	  \leanok
2602	  \uses{def:presentation_chart, def:fiber_rank,
2603	    lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank}
2604	  A point \(s \in W_e\) lies in some \(e\)-presentation chart \(V\), so
2605	  \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is generated by \(e\) elements.  Its fiber
2606	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak p_s) \otimes \Gamma(\F, V)\) at the prime of \(s\) is
2607	  then spanned by \(e\) vectors, so the fiber rank at \(\mathfrak p_s\)
2608	  (\cref{def:fiber_rank}) --- the point rank of \(\F\) at \(s\), by
2609	  \cref{lem:point_rank_eq_chart_fiber_rank} --- is at most \(e\).
2610	\end{proof}

2612	\begin{lemma}[The restricted module is chart-covered]
2613	  \label{lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}
2614	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.chartsCover_chartLocus}
2615	  \leanok
2616	  \uses{def:chart_locus, def:charts_cover, lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2617	  Let \(\F\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.  The restriction of
2618	  \(\F\) to the open subscheme \(W_e\) --- its pullback along the open
2619	  immersion \(W_e \hookrightarrow S\) --- admits \(e\)-presentation charts.

2622	\begin{proof}
2623	  \leanok
2624	  \uses{lem:presentation_pullback_sections, def:presentation_chart}
2625	  Every point of \(W_e\) lies in some \(e\)-presentation chart
2626	  \(V \subseteq W_e\).  The preimage of \(V\) in the open subscheme is an
2627	  affine open mapped isomorphically onto \(V\), and by
2628	  \cref{lem:presentation_pullback_sections} the section module of the
2629	  restricted sheaf over it inherits a matrix presentation by \(e\)
2630	  generators.
2631	\end{proof}

2633	\subsection{The rank stratum over the chart locus}
2634	
2635	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(e\) stratum over the base]
2636	  \label{def:rank_stratum}
2637	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.rankStratum}
2638	  \leanok
2639	  \uses{def:stratum, def:chart_locus, lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}
2640	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2641	  The \emph{rank-\(e\) stratum} \(S_e\) of a quasi-coherent
2642	  \(\OO_S\)-module \(\F\) is the rank-\(e\) stratum (\cref{def:stratum}) of
2643	  the restriction of \(\F\) to the \(e\)-chart locus \(W_e\), which admits
2644	  \(e\)-presentation charts by \cref{lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}: a
2645	  closed subscheme of the open subscheme \(W_e\), hence a locally closed
2646	  subscheme of \(S\).
2647	\end{definition}
2648	
2649	\begin{definition}[Immersion of the rank-\(e\) stratum]
2650	  \label{def:rank_stratum_immersion}
2651	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.rankStratumŒπ}
2652	  \leanok
2653	  \uses{def:rank_stratum, def:stratum_immersion, def:chart_locus}
2654	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2655	  The canonical morphism \(\iota_e : S_e \to S\) is the composite of the
2656	  closed immersion \(S_e \hookrightarrow W_e\)
2657	  (\cref{def:stratum_immersion}) with the open immersion
2658	  \(W_e \hookrightarrow S\); it is an immersion.
2659	\end{definition}
2660	
2661	\begin{theorem}[Support of the rank-\(e\) stratum over the base]
2662	  \label{thm:mem_range_rank_stratum}
2663	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.mem_range_rankStratumŒπ_iff}
2664	  \leanok
2665	  \uses{def:rank_stratum_immersion, def:point_rank}
2666	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2667	  Let \(S\) be locally noetherian and \(\F\) finitely presented.  A point
2668	  \(s \in S\) lies in the image of \(\iota_e : S_e \to S\) if and only if
2669	  the point rank of \(\F\) at \(s\) equals \(e\).
2670	\end{theorem}
2671	
2672	\begin{proof}
2673	  \leanok
2674	  \uses{thm:mem_range_stratum, thm:point_rank_pullback,
2675	    lem:chart_locus_contains_rank_locus}
2676	  Write \(\F' := \F|_{W_e}\) for the restriction to the chart locus; by
2677	  \cref{thm:point_rank_pullback} (applied to the open immersion) the point
2678	  rank of \(\F'\) at a point of \(W_e\) equals the point rank of \(\F\) at
2679	  its image in \(S\).
2680	
2681	  If \(s = \iota_e(z)\), then \(s \in W_e\) and \(s\), viewed in \(W_e\),
2682	  lies in the support of the stratum of \(\F'\), so the point rank of
2683	  \(\F'\) there is \(e\) (\cref{thm:mem_range_stratum}); hence the point
2684	  rank of \(\F\) at \(s\) is \(e\).  Conversely, if the point rank of
2685	  \(\F\) at \(s\) is \(e\), then \(s \in W_e\)
2686	  (\cref{lem:chart_locus_contains_rank_locus}); the point rank of \(\F'\)
2687	  at \(s\) is \(e\), so \(s\) lies in the support of the stratum
2688	  (\cref{thm:mem_range_stratum}), i.e.\ in the image of \(\iota_e\).
2689	\end{proof}
2690	
2691	\begin{theorem}[The rank-\(e\) stratum flattens \(\F\)]
2692	  \label{thm:rank_stratum_flattens}
2693	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.coherentSheafFlat_rankStratum}
2694	  \leanok
2695	  \uses{def:rank_stratum, def:rank_stratum_immersion,
2696	    def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2697	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2698	  Let \(\F\) be a quasi-coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.  The pullback
2699	  \(\iota_e^*\F\) is flat over \(S_e\).
2700	\end{theorem}
2701	
2702	\begin{proof}
2703	  \leanok
2704	  \uses{thm:stratum_flattens, lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}
2705	  Since \(\iota_e\) is the composite of the closed immersion
2706	  \(\iota : S_e \to W_e\) with the open immersion \(W_e \to S\), pullback
2707	  functoriality gives \(\iota_e^*\F \cong \iota^*(\F|_{W_e})\).  The
2708	  restriction \(\F|_{W_e}\) admits \(e\)-presentation charts
2709	  (\cref{lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}), so \(\iota^*(\F|_{W_e})\) is flat
2710	  over \(S_e\) by \cref{thm:stratum_flattens}; flatness is invariant under
2711	  isomorphism of modules.
2712	\end{proof}
2713	
2714	\subsection{Rank bound, open rank fibers, and the single-stratum
2715	  factorization}
2716	
2717	\begin{lemma}[The point rank is bounded on a noetherian scheme]
2718	  \label{lem:point_rank_bounded}
2719	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_pointRank_le}
2720	  \leanok
2721	  \uses{def:point_rank}
2722	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2723	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a finitely presented
2724	  \(\OO_S\)-module.  There is an \(N\) such that the point rank of \(\F\)
2725	  is at most \(N\) at every point of \(S\).
2726	\end{lemma}
2727	
2728	\begin{proof}
2729	  \leanok
2730	  \uses{thm:presentation_chart_exists, def:chart_locus,
2731	    lem:chart_locus_rank_bound}
2732	  For each \(s \in S\) choose an \(e(s)\)-presentation chart \(V_s \ni s\)
2733	  with \(e(s)\) the point rank at \(s\)
2734	  (\cref{thm:presentation_chart_exists}).  The noetherian space \(S\) is
2735	  quasi-compact, so finitely many charts \(V_{s_1}, \dots, V_{s_k}\) cover
2736	  \(S\); set \(N := \max_i e(s_i)\).  Any \(x \in S\) lies in some
2737	  \(V_{s_i}\), hence in the \(e(s_i)\)-chart locus, so its point rank is at
2738	  most \(e(s_i) \le N\) (\cref{lem:chart_locus_rank_bound}).
2739	\end{proof}
2740	
2741	\begin{theorem}[Openness of the rank fibers of a flat pullback]
2742	  \label{thm:rank_fibers_open}
2743	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isOpen_pointRank_pullback_eq}
2744	  \leanok
2745	  \uses{def:point_rank, def:coherent_sheaf_flat,
2746	    lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}
2747	  Let \(S\) be locally noetherian, \(\F\) a finitely presented
2748	  \(\OO_S\)-module, and \(\varphi : T \to S\) a morphism with
2749	  \(\varphi^*\F\) flat over \(T\).  For every \(e\), the locus of points of
2750	  \(T\) at which the point rank of \(\varphi^*\F\) equals \(e\) is open in
2751	  \(T\).
2752	\end{theorem}
2753	
2754	\begin{proof}
2755	  \leanok
2756	  \uses{lem:affine_finite_sections_nhds, lem:exists_matrix_presentation,
2757	    lem:presentation_pullback_sections, lem:matrix_presentation_fp,
2758	    lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat, thm:stalk_rank_eq_point_rank}
2759	  Let \(t_0\) be a point of the locus.  Choose an affine open
2760	  \(V \ni \varphi(t_0)\) on which \(\Gamma(\F, V)\) is finite over the
2761	  noetherian ring \(\Gamma(S, V)\)
2762	  (\cref{lem:affine_finite_sections_nhds}), hence finitely presented, and
2763	  an affine open \(W \ni t_0\) with \(W \subseteq \varphi^{-1}(V)\).  Set
2764	  \(M := \Gamma(\varphi^*\F, W)\) and \(R := \Gamma(T, W)\).  Then \(M\) is
2765	  flat over \(R\) (\cref{lem:flat_sections_of_sheaf_flat}) and finitely
2766	  presented: a matrix presentation of \(\Gamma(\F, V)\)
2767	  (\cref{lem:exists_matrix_presentation}) pulls back to a matrix
2768	  presentation of \(M\) (\cref{lem:presentation_pullback_sections}), and a
2769	  module with a matrix presentation is finitely presented
2770	  (\cref{lem:matrix_presentation_fp}).
2771	
2772	  A flat, finitely presented module is locally free, so the function
2773	  \(\mathfrak p \mapsto \operatorname{rk} M_{\mathfrak p}\) is locally
2774	  constant on the prime spectrum of \(R\).  Under the canonical
2775	  identification of \(W\) with that spectrum, this function is the point
2776	  rank of \(\varphi^*\F\) (\cref{thm:stalk_rank_eq_point_rank}).  Hence the
2777	  set of points of \(W\) of point rank \(e\) is open in \(W\) and contains
2778	  \(t_0\); as \(W\) is open in \(T\), the locus is a neighbourhood of each
2779	  of its points.
2780	\end{proof}
2781	
2782	\begin{theorem}[Unique factorization through the rank-\(e\) stratum of the
2783	  base]
2784	  \label{thm:factor_rank_stratum_unique}
2785	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.existsUnique_factor_rankStratum}
2786	  \leanok
2787	  \uses{def:rank_stratum, def:rank_stratum_immersion,
2788	    def:coherent_sheaf_flat, def:point_rank}
2789	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2790	  Let \(S\) be locally noetherian, \(\F\) a finitely presented
2791	  \(\OO_S\)-module, and \(\varphi : T \to S\) a morphism such that
2792	  \(\varphi^*\F\) is flat over \(T\) and the point rank of \(\F\) at
2793	  \(\varphi(t)\) equals \(e\) for every \(t \in T\).  Then \(\varphi\)
2794	  factors uniquely through \(\iota_e : S_e \to S\).
2795	\end{theorem}
2796	
2797	\begin{proof}
2798	  \leanok
2799	  \uses{lem:chart_locus_contains_rank_locus, thm:point_rank_pullback,
2800	    lem:charts_cover_chart_locus, thm:stratum_lift_unique}
2801	  Every \(\varphi(t)\) has point rank \(e\), hence lies in the chart locus
2802	  \(W_e\) (\cref{lem:chart_locus_contains_rank_locus}), so \(\varphi\)
2803	  factors through the open immersion \(W_e \hookrightarrow S\) as
2804	  \(\varphi = (W_e \hookrightarrow S) \circ q\) with \(q : T \to W_e\).
2805	  Write \(\F' := \F|_{W_e}\); it admits \(e\)-presentation charts
2806	  (\cref{lem:charts_cover_chart_locus}).  By functoriality of pullback,
2807	  \(q^*\F' \cong \varphi^*\F\) is flat over \(T\), and by
2808	  \cref{thm:point_rank_pullback} (applied twice, to \(q\) and to the open
2809	  immersion) its point rank at \(t\) equals the point rank of \(\F\) at
2810	  \(\varphi(t)\), namely \(e\).  Now \cref{thm:stratum_lift_unique} gives a
2811	  unique \(l : T \to S_e\) with \(\iota \circ l = q\) for the closed
2812	  immersion \(\iota : S_e \to W_e\); composing,
2813	  \(\iota_e \circ l = \varphi\).
2814	
2815	  For uniqueness, let \(l' : T \to S_e\) satisfy
2816	  \(\iota_e \circ l' = \varphi\).  Then \(\iota \circ l'\) and \(q\) are two
2817	  factorizations of \(\varphi\) through the open immersion
2818	  \(W_e \hookrightarrow S\), which is a monomorphism, so
2819	  \(\iota \circ l' = q\); by the uniqueness in
2820	  \cref{thm:stratum_lift_unique}, \(l' = l\).
2821	\end{proof}

2823	\section{Universal property of the strata}
2824	\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}
2825	
2826	\begin{theorem}[Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (case
2827	  \(n = 0\))]
2828	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
2829	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}
2830	  \leanok
2831	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2832	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}

2834	  Then there exist a finite index set \(I\) and locally-closed immersions
2835	  \(\iota_f : S_f \to S\) (\(f \in I\)) with pairwise disjoint images
2836	  set-theoretically covering \(|S|\), such that the pullback of \(\F\)
2837	  along \(\iota_f\) is flat over \(S_f\) for every \(f\) (in the sense of
2838	  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}), and such that, writing
2839	  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and \(i : S' \to S\) for the morphism
2840	  assembled from the \(\iota_f\), the pair \((S', i)\) has the universal
2841	  property: every morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) for which
2842	  \(\varphi^* \F\) is flat over \(T\) factors \emph{uniquely} through
2843	  \(i\).
2844	
2845	  This is part (ii) of Nitsure's flattening-stratification theorem in the
2846	  special case \(n = 0\), stated with flatness in place of local freeness of
2847	  constant rank: for a finitely presented module the two notions agree
2848	  locally, the rank decomposing \(T\) into clopen pieces.  Nitsure states an
2849	  ``if and only if''; the converse direction --- any morphism factoring
2850	  through \(i\) has flat pullback --- is stability of flatness under base
2851	  change (\cref{lem:sheaf_flat_pullback}) and is not part of this statement.
2852	  The universal property for a relative family (Nitsure's part (ii) for
2853	  \(\F\) on \(\PP^n_S\), hence for projective \(\pi : X \to S\))

2856	  for a general \emph{proper} family the universal property is not asserted
2857	  here, and the reduced strata of \cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} do
2858	  not witness it (over \(S = \Spec k[\varepsilon]\) with \(\F = \OO_S\)

2920	\section{Application: relative curves}
2921	\label{sec:flatstrat_curve_application}
2922	
2923	In the Route~A consumer setting, the input to the flattening-stratification
2924	machinery is the pullback \(C \times_k T \to T\) where \(C\) is a smooth proper
2925	curve over a field \(k\) and \(T\) is any \(k\)-scheme.  The hypotheses of
2926	Nitsure's \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} demand
2927	\emph{projective} relative dimension, not merely \emph{proper}; we record that
2928	no extra work is required to instantiate the theorem in our setting.
2929	
2930	\begin{lemma}
2931	[Smooth proper curve over \(k\) is projective]
2932	  \label{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}
2933	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.smoothProperCurveProjective}
2934	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6 (curve \(\Rightarrow\) quasi-projective;
2935	  % proper \(\Rightarrow\) projective for curves), and standard consequences in
2936	  % chapter III. The precise statement ``every smooth proper curve over a field
2937	  % is projective'' is one of the classical equivalences for \(\dim 1\) and is
2938	  % textbook material; this block is project-bespoke (no verbatim source
2939	  % quote is reproduced here as the result is folklore-level and cited only
2940	  % structurally for the Route~A reduction).
2941	  \textit{Source: classical; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.6.7 \& II.7.6 (curves).}
2942	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\).  Then \(C \to \Spec k\) is
2943	  projective.  Consequently, for any noetherian \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the relative
2944	  curve \(C \times_k T \to T\) is projective in the sense of Grothendieck (there
2945	  exists a relatively very ample line bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\)).
2946	\end{lemma}
2947	
2948	\begin{proof}
2949	  Projectivity of a smooth proper curve over a field is classical
2950	  ([Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6; alternatively: every divisor of large
2951	  positive degree on a smooth proper curve \(C\) is very ample, providing the
2952	  embedding \(C \hookrightarrow \PP^N_k\) for some \(N\)). Pulling the ample line
2953	  bundle back along \(C \times_k T \to C\) produces a relatively very ample line
2954	  bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\).
2955	\end{proof}
2956	
2957	\begin{corollary}
2958	
2959	\leanok
2960	[Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative curve]
2961	  \label{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}
2962	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
2963	  \uses{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective, thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
2964	  Let \(k\) be a field, \(C\) a smooth proper curve over \(k\), and \(T\) a noetherian
2965	  \(k\)-scheme.  Let \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on \(C \times_k T\). Then the
2966	  flattening-stratification conclusion of
2967	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} applies: there is a finite
2968	  set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials and locally-closed subschemes
2969	  \(T_f \hookrightarrow T\) for \(f \in I\), set-theoretically covering \(T\)
2970	  disjointly, such that (a) \(\F|_{C \times_k T_f}\) is flat over \(T_f\) for
2971	  each \(f\), and (b) any morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) such that
2972	  \(\varphi^* \F\) on \(C \times_k T'\) is \(T'\)-flat factors uniquely through some
2973	  \(T_f\).
2974	\end{corollary}
2975	
2976	\begin{proof}
2977	  
2978	  By \cref{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}, \(C \times_k T \to T\) is
2979	  projective, hence factors through a closed immersion into \(\PP^n_T\) for some
2980	  \(n \ge 0\).  Push \(\F\) forward along the closed immersion (extending by zero
2981	  outside the image) to a coherent sheaf \(\tilde \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\); this
2982	  operation preserves \(T\)-flatness in both directions because the closed
2983	  immersion is a finite morphism.  Apply
2984	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} to \(\tilde \F\) on
2985	  \(\PP^n_T\) to obtain \(\{T_f\}\) with the stated universal property.
2986	\end{proof}

3002	iso-insensitivity of the flatness predicate
3003	(\texttt{coherentSheafFlat\_of\_comp\_isIso}) and empty-scheme padding of
3004	the index set.  The \(n = 0\) universal property

904	    have hj := (mem_range_rankStratumŒπ_iff F j.down.1 s).mp hsj

287	theorem strataData_le_ker (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ‚ü∂ X)
288	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ùüô T)
289	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
290	    (hrank : ‚àÄ t : T,
291	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫
292	      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
293	    strataData G e hcov ‚â§ q.ker := by
294	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫
295	  refine Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_ofIdeals_iff.mpr fun U => ?_
296	  intro r hr
297	  rw [RingHom.mem_ker]
298	  -- a finite family of `e`-presentation charts covering `U`
299	  obtain ‚ü®N, Vc, mmc, Pc, hVcU, hcovU‚ü© :=
300	    ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts G hcov U
301	  -- the pulled-back section dies on an affine neighbourhood of each point
302	  apply T.IsSheaf.section_ext fun t ht => ?_
303	  obtain ‚ü®i, hti‚ü© := hcovU (q.base t) ht
304	  have htVc : t ‚àà q ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (Vc i).1 := hti
305	  obtain ‚ü®W, hW, htW, hWle‚ü© :=
306	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := t) (U := q ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (Vc i).1) htVc
307	  have hV : W ‚â§ q ‚Åª¬π·µÅ U.1 := fun y hy => hVcU i (hWle hy)
308	  refine ‚ü®W, hV, htW, ?_‚ü©
309	  -- the relation matrix of the chart pulls back to zero on `W`
310	  haveI hFlatW : Module.Flat Œì(T, W)
311	      Œì((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
312	    flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id hflat hW
313	  obtain ‚ü®P', hP'‚ü© := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections q G
314	    (Vc i).2 hW hWle (Pc i)
315	  haveI : Module.Finite Œì(T, W) Œì((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
316	    Module.Finite.of_surjective P'.proj P'.surjective_proj
317	  have h0 : P'.relMatrix = 0 :=
318	    P'.relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat fun p =>
319	      (rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank _ hW p).trans (hrank _)
320	  -- hence the entry ideal is killed by `q.appLE (Vc i) W`
321	  letI : Algebra Œì(X, (Vc i).1) Œì(T, W) := (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom.toAlgebra
322	  have hker : (Pc i).entryIdeal ‚â§
323	      RingHom.ker (algebraMap Œì(X, (Vc i).1) Œì(T, W)) :=
324	    (_root_.Module.MatrixPresentation.relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff
325	      (A := Œì(T, W))).mp (hP' ‚ñ∏ h0)
326	  -- the strata-ideal condition at the chart
327	  have hmem : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r ‚àà (Pc i).entryIdeal :=
328	    (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr (Vc i) (hVcU i) (mmc i) (Pc i)
329	  have hzero : (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom
330	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r) = 0 :=
331	    RingHom.mem_ker.mp (hker hmem)
332	  -- assemble: restriction of `q.app U r` to `W` is `q.appLE (Vc i) W` of
333	  -- the restricted section
334	  have hres : (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
335	      (q.appLE U.1 W hV).hom r := rfl
336	  show (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
337	    (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom 0
338	  rw [map_zero, hres, ‚Üê appLE_base_res_apply' q (hVcU i) hWle hV r, hzero]
339	
340	/-- **Unique factorization through the rank-`e` stratum** [Nitsure ¬ß4,
341	part (ii), `n = 0` case]: a morphism `q : T ‚ü∂ X` whose pulled-back module
342	is flat over `T` of constant point rank `e` factors uniquely through the
343	closed immersion of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
344	theorem existsUnique_stratumLift (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ‚ü∂ X)
345	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ùüô T)
346	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
347	    (hrank : ‚àÄ t : T,
348	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫
349	      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
350	    ‚àÉ! l : T ‚ü∂ stratum G e hcov, l ‚â´ stratumŒπ G e hcov = q := by
351	  have hker : (stratumŒπ G e hcov).ker ‚â§ q.ker := by
352	    have h1 : (stratumŒπ G e hcov).ker = strataData G e hcov :=
353	      Scheme.IdealSheafData.ker_subschemeŒπ (strataData G e hcov)
354	    rw [h1]
355	    exact strataData_le_ker G hcov q hflat hrank
356	  refine ‚ü®IsClosedImmersion.lift (stratumŒπ G e hcov) q hker,

770	theorem existsUnique_factor_rankStratum [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
771	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (e : ‚Ñï) {T : Scheme.{u}} (œÜ : T ‚ü∂ S)
772	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ùüô T)
773	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback œÜ).obj F))
774	    (hrank : ‚àÄ t : T, pointRank S F (œÜ.base t) = e) :
775	    ‚àÉ! l : T ‚ü∂ rankStratum F e, l ‚â´ rankStratumŒπ F e = œÜ := by
776	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).Œπ F inferInstance
777	  -- `œÜ` lands in the chart locus

779	    rintro _ ‚ü®t, rfl‚ü©

782	    have h2 : (chartLocus F e : Set S) ‚äÜ Set.range (chartLocus F e).Œπ.base := by
783	      rw [Scheme.Opens.range_Œπ]
784	    exact h2 h1

786	    IsOpenImmersion.lift (chartLocus F e).Œπ œÜ hrange with hq
787	  have hqfac : q ‚â´ (chartLocus F e).Œπ = œÜ :=
788	    IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac (chartLocus F e).Œπ œÜ hrange
789	  -- flatness of the pullback along `q`

791	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj
792	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).Œπ).obj F)) := by
793	    intro U hU V hV eV
794	    exact coherentSheafFlat_of_iso (ùüô T)
795	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp q (chartLocus F e).Œπ).app F ‚â™‚â´
796	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hqfac).app F).symm) hflat hU hV eV
797	  -- constant rank of the pullback along `q`

799	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q
800	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).Œπ).obj F) ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫

802	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).Œπ).obj F)) t = e := by

812	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).Œπ).obj F)
